meta.name: Cast-Iron Skillet Cornbread
meta.subtitle: Crisp edges • tender crumb • sweet or savory variants • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: Canonical skillet cornbread baked in a preheated cast-iron pan for crisp edges. Supports classic unsweetened Southern style and a sweet variant.

tasteProfile.notes: Corn-forward flavor, crisp buttery edges from the hot skillet, tender interior. Sweet or savory depending on add-ins.

ingredients.label: Cornmeal (medium grind preferred; or fresh milled cornmeal—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: Stone-ground gives more flavor; fine grind yields a smoother crumb.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (optional for tenderness; omit for 100% corn cornbread)
ingredients.notes: Southern-style can be all-cornmeal; adding a little flour makes the crumb a bit lighter.

ingredients.label: Baking soda
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 teaspoon. Works with buttermilk for lift and browning.

ingredients.label: Sugar (optional for sweet cornbread)
ingredients.notes: 0g for classic savory; 25–60g depending on sweetness preference.

ingredients.label: Buttermilk (cold or room temp)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/4 cups. If you don’t have buttermilk, see Variations for substitutes.

ingredients.label: Melted butter (or neutral oil) for batter
ingredients.notes: 4 tbsp melted butter. Neutral oil works too.

ingredients.label: Butter (or bacon drippings/neutral oil) for hot skillet
ingredients.notes: 2 tbsp. This creates the signature crisp crust.

steps.title: Preheat oven and skillet
instructions: Place the cast-iron skillet in the oven to preheat for 10 minutes.
Preheating the skillet is key for crisp edges.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
instructions: In a bowl, whisk cornmeal, flour (if using), baking powder, baking soda, salt, and sugar (if using).

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
instructions: In a second bowl (or a well in the dry bowl), whisk buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter/oil until smooth.

steps.title: Combine batter (don’t overmix)
instructions: Pour wet into dry and stir just until combined. A few small lumps are fine.
If using add-ins (cheddar, jalapeño, corn), fold them in now.

steps.title: Add fat to hot skillet + pour batter
instructions: Carefully remove the hot skillet from the oven.
Add skillet fat (butter/bacon drippings/oil) and swirl to coat the bottom and sides. It should sizzle.
Immediately pour batter into the skillet (you should hear a sizzle—this helps crust).

steps.title: Bake
instructions: Bake 18–24 minutes until golden and set in the center.
A toothpick should come out clean or with a few moist crumbs.

steps.title: Cool briefly and serve
instructions: Cool 5–10 minutes before slicing for best structure.
Serve warm with butter, honey (sweet variant), or alongside beans/greens/chili (savory).

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled cornmeal is flavorful but can be coarser and thirstier. Hydration and a short soak can improve tenderness.
guidelines: If milling your own cornmeal, aim for a medium grind. Very coarse meal can taste gritty unless soaked.
Optional for tenderness: soak cornmeal in the buttermilk for 10–20 minutes before mixing (a quick 'soaker').
Fresh milled cornmeal can absorb more liquid; if batter looks very thick, add 15–30g extra buttermilk.
Ifecommended beginner blend: use 60g flour for a lighter crumb; omit flour for a more rustic, all-corn texture.

storage.counter: Best same day. Store airtight at room temp up to 1 day; rewarm for best texture.
storage.fridge: Up to 4 days airtight. Rewarm in skillet/toaster oven.
storage.freezer: Freeze wedges up to 2 months. Thaw and rewarm at 350°F (175°C) 8–12 minutes.

variations.name: Classic savory Southern (no sugar, bacon drippings)
variations.change: Omit sugar entirely. Use bacon drippings for skillet fat (28g).
variations.notes: Great with beans, greens, gumbo, or chili.

variations.name: Sweet cornbread
variations.change: Use 50–60g sugar. Optionally reduce salt to 5g. Brush top with melted butter after baking.

variations.name: No buttermilk substitute
variations.change: Use 300g milk + 15g lemon juice or vinegar. Let sit 5 minutes before mixing.
variations.notes: Flavor slightly different, still good lift.

variations.name: Jalapeño-cheddar skillet cornbread
variations.change: Fold in 30g jalapeño + 80g cheddar. Optionally add 80g corn kernels.
variations.notes: If batter seems too thick, add 10–20g extra buttermilk.

variations.name: 100% cornmeal (no flour)
variations.change: Omit flour. Consider soaking cornmeal in buttermilk 10–20 minutes before mixing.
variations.notes: More rustic texture; still excellent in cast iron.

notes: Hot skillet fat + immediate batter pour = crisp crust. Don’t let the skillet cool before adding batter.
Avoid overmixing; quick breads get tough if stirred too much.
If you want extra crisp edges, bake in cast iron and use butter or drippings as skillet fat.

audit.testNotes: Canonical cast-iron cornbread with crisp crust. Supports savory Southern style and sweet variant via optional sugar and add-ins.
